page: Alibaba Cloud ecs.hfg5.2xlarge is a general-purpose virtual machine instance powered by the KVM hypervisor. It features 8 dedicated vCPUs mapped to 8 physical cores of an Intel Xeon 6149 processor running at 3.1 GHz. The instance is equipped with 32.0 GB of system memory, providing a balanced 4.0 GB of RAM per core. It does not include local storage or dedicated GPU hardware, relying on network-attached storage and a baseline network bandwidth of 2 Gbps.

Performance benchmarks indicate a mix of average and weak results. The Intel Xeon processor delivers average multi-core CPU performance under stress-ng and average scores in Passmark CPU tests, including extended instructions and encryption. However, single-core performance is weak, as shown by stress-ng and Geekbench single-core metrics. Memory bandwidth is average for small cached blocks but drops to weak for large uncached blocks. In application-level benchmarks, the instance achieves average performance in Redis SET operations and small-model LLM inference, but exhibits poor performance for medium-sized LLM models and weak throughput in static web serving and database operations.

From a cost-efficiency perspective, the ecs.hfg5.2xlarge offers dedicated physical cores without hyperthreading, which provides predictable compute performance for general-purpose workloads. However, the lack of local storage and modest network bandwidth are key tradeoffs to consider. This instance is best suited for general-purpose workloads, basic web serving, text processing, software compilation, and lightweight application hosting where dedicated CPU allocation is required but extreme single-core speed or high storage I/O is not critical.
description: Alibaba Cloud ecs.hfg5.2xlarge is a general-purpose instance featuring 8 dedicated vCPUs on an Intel Xeon 6149 processor running at 3.1 GHz, paired with 32.0 GB of RAM. Operating on the KVM hypervisor, this x86_64 instance lacks local storage and GPU accelerators, utilizing a baseline network bandwidth of 2 Gbps. Benchmark data shows average multi-core CPU performance and memory bandwidth for cached blocks, though single-core performance and uncached memory bandwidth are weak. The instance performs average in Redis operations and small-model LLM inference, but shows poor results with larger models. It represents a balanced resource profile with dedicated physical cores, making it suitable for general-purpose applications, software compilation, and text processing workloads that do not require high local storage I/O.
og_description: Alibaba Cloud ecs.hfg5.2xlarge is a general-purpose instance featuring 8 dedicated Intel Xeon vCPUs, 32 GB RAM, and a 2 Gbps network baseline, suitable for balanced workloads.
meta_description: Alibaba Cloud ecs.hfg5.2xlarge features 8 dedicated Intel Xeon vCPUs and 32 GB RAM, delivering balanced general-purpose compute.
tagline: A dedicated eight-core x86_64 compute instance with balanced memory allocation for general-purpose workloads and standard application hosting.
bullet_points: 8 dedicated Intel Xeon 6149 vCPUs running at 3.1 GHz
32.0 GB of system memory with 4.0 GB per core
KVM hypervisor with 2 Gbps baseline network bandwidth
No bundled local storage or GPU accelerators
Average multi-core CPU and cached memory performance
Best suited for general-purpose applications and software compilation
